description: Toolchain verification run: confirmed init_experiment/run_experiment/log_experiment and METRIC parsing work.

asi.hypothesis: Autoresearch toolchain is callable and parses structured metrics.
asi.rollback_reason: Verification run only; no optimization changes to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Set up autoresearch.md/autoresearch.sh and run real baseline.

description: Baseline harness run: JIT-enabled build fails in compemu_support include path with many unresolved AArch64 codegen macros/types before smoke tests can start.

asi.hypothesis: Current AArch64 JIT backend wiring should compile and then fail at runtime smoke.
asi.rollback_reason: No score improvement (0) and build gate failed; cannot evaluate runtime modes.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix compile-time AArch64 codegen integration first (STATIC_INLINE/LENDFUNC macro context and regstruct field expectations), then rerun baseline.
asi.build_failure_signature: codegen_arm64.cpp errors: STATIC_INLINE undefined, LENDFUNC parse failures, missing regs.raw_cputbl_count, undefined compemu_raw_* symbols from compemu_support.cpp include.

description: Reconfirmed baseline: build gate still fails before smoke tests, score remains 0.

asi.hypothesis: Current trunk state still reproduces the compile-time AArch64 JIT integration failure with no runtime smoke coverage.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect the generated/codegen include ordering and missing macro/type context around compemu_support.cpp and codegen_arm64.cpp, then patch the compile blockers.

description: Tried importing missing AArch64 helper shims and write-window scaffolding; build still fails earlier because arm JIT support macros (STATIC_INLINE/LENDFUNC/MENDFUNC) are absent and the backend still assumes non-Basilisk symbols like regs.raw_cputbl_count.

asi.hypothesis: Providing AArch64-local helper aliases, write-window no-ops, and countdown/emulated_ticks fixes would clear the first compile blockers.
asi.rollback_reason: No score improvement; the compile now fails earlier on missing arm JIT macro infrastructure and remaining backend assumptions.
asi.next_action_hint: Add the missing arm JIT macro definitions/overloads in compemu.h/compemu_support.cpp, retarget compemu_raw_inc_opcount to the global raw_cputbl_count array, and rerun to expose the next genuine backend blockers.
asi.failure_signature: STATIC_INLINE/LENDFUNC/MENDFUNC undefined in compemu.h, compemu_midfunc_arm64[_2].cpp expects 1-arg readreg/writereg/rmw overloads, codegen_arm64.cpp references non-existent regs.raw_cputbl_count.

description: Added missing arm-style inline/midfunc macros and several AArch64 helper shims; compile now gets substantially deeper, surfacing Basilisk-specific regstruct/global mismatches and many still-missing arm64 midfunc/codegen entry points.

asi.hypothesis: Restoring the arm JIT macro layer plus a few AArch64 storage/flag helper shims would unblock parsing and expose the next real backend incompatibilities.
asi.rollback_reason: No score improvement yet; build still fails before producing a binary.
asi.next_action_hint: Port the remaining Basilisk-vs-Amiberry assumptions next: replace regstruct field references (raw_cputbl_count/jit_exception/cache_tags/mem_banks) with actual Basilisk globals or helpers, and either implement or prune the missing arm64 midfunc/codegen API used by compemu_support.cpp.
asi.failure_signature: Deeper compile now reaches codegen_arm64/compemu_midfunc_arm64_2 and fails on missing Basilisk fields/globals (jit_exception, cache_tags, mem_banks, baseaddr, currprefs), missing prepare_for_call/readreg_specific overloads, and many absent arm64 helper entry points referenced by compemu_support.cpp.

description: Defined the missing arm inline macro layer plus 64-bit pointer mode for AArch64 and basic metadata shims; this removes the earlier pointer-truncation failures and narrows the remaining blockers to arm64-midfunc regstruct assumptions plus a missing generic helper surface in compemu_support.cpp.

asi.hypothesis: Teaching the 2021 JIT headers about AArch64/64-bit pointer mode and predeclaring the arm64-only metadata globals would unblock the larger helper API mismatch cleanly.
asi.rollback_reason: Still no build_ok improvement; compile now fails on a smaller but still broad set of unimplemented generic helper wrappers and a few remaining arm64 field assumptions.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a dedicated AArch64 shim layer for the generic helper API used by compemu_support.cpp (`add_l_ri`, `and_l`, `mov_b_rm`, `mov_w_mr`, stack helpers, indexed load/store wrappers, cmp/jnz/jmp helpers) and rewrite `compemu_midfunc_arm64_2.cpp` / `codegen_arm64.cpp` to use global `jit_exception`, `cache_tags`, and `mem_banks` instead of nonexistent regstruct members.
asi.failure_signature: CPU_64_BIT path now activates, but compemu.h still needs <cstdint> for uintptr_t; arm64_2 still references regs.jit_exception and regs.mem_banks; compemu_support.cpp now mainly fails on missing generic helper wrappers such as bsf_l_rr/simulate_bsf/add_l_ri/and_l/mov_b_rm/mov_w_mr/test_l_ri/raw_dec_sp/compemu_raw_and_l_ri/compemu_raw_jmp_m_indexed/raw_init_cpu.

description: Tried the cleaner architecture split: redirected AArch64 builds to a new arm-specific support unit derived from Amiberry instead of patching the x86-oriented compemu_support.cpp path.

asi.hypothesis: Using an ARM-specific support engine plus a small Basilisk compatibility layer would bypass the exploding generic-helper shim surface in the monolithic support file.
asi.rollback_reason: Still no build_ok improvement; the imported ARM support compiles further but exposes a second adaptation layer that Basilisk lacks (prefs/runtime typedefs, regstruct metadata fields, and arm64 backend globals/prototypes).
asi.next_action_hint: If continuing this path, land a focused compatibility layer first: shared AArch64 prefs typedefs (`currprefs`/`changed_prefs`), `flags_carry_inverted`/`clobber_flags`/readreg declarations visible to codegen_arm64, and regstruct or global replacements for `raw_cputbl_count`, `jit_exception`, `cache_tags`, `countdown`/`emulated_ticks`, and `NATMEM_OFFSET`.
asi.failure_signature: Alternate compemu_support_arm path parses past the generic-helper gap, but then fails on missing Basilisk ARM compatibility pieces: `_T`/`uae_bswap_32`, `flags_carry_inverted`, `clobber_flags`, `readreg`, regstruct fields (`raw_cputbl_count`, `jit_exception`, `cache_tags`), and arm64 midfunc prototype visibility.

description: Retried the arm-specific support split with a first Basilisk compatibility layer (64-bit pointer mode, minimal prefs globals, regstruct metadata fields, and AArch64 redirection in compemu_support.cpp).

asi.hypothesis: A small shared compatibility layer on top of the imported ARM support file would satisfy the missing typedefs/globals and get the build past the initial integration failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Still no build_ok improvement; compile moved further but now clearly fails on deeper Basilisk-vs-Amiberry runtime ABI mismatches inside the imported ARM support path.
asi.next_action_hint: If pursuing the split path, systematically reconcile the runtime ABI next: add/import the AArch64 popall symbols and countdown mapping, replace `MEMBaseDiff`/`NATMEM_OFFSET` assumptions with Basilisk direct-addressing equivalents, patch imported support code to Basilisk's actual `regstruct`/FPU layout (`fp_result`, scratch regs), and either stub or port `special_mem`, `jit_n_addr_unsafe`, `nfctbl`, and related memory/JIT state helpers.
asi.failure_signature: Support-arm path now gets through the early typedef/prototype issues and then fails on concrete ABI gaps: missing popall *_setpc symbols, missing `countdown`/usable `NATMEM_OFFSET`, absent `baseaddr`/`special_mem`/`jit_n_addr_unsafe`, Amiberry-only regstruct fields (`scratchregs`, `scratchfregs`, `fp_result.fp`), and missing UAE helper tables like `nfctbl` and `raw_cputbl_count` wiring.

description: Extended the arm-specific support split with a larger Basilisk compatibility layer; this now gets all the way through compilation into the final link step before failing on missing exported JIT/runtime wrapper symbols.

asi.hypothesis: The imported ARM support path can be made buildable with a focused compatibility shim, and once compile-time ABI mismatches are patched the next blockers would be linkable wrapper/entrypoint gaps rather than parser issues.
asi.rollback_reason: Still no build_ok improvement; despite much deeper progress, the binary does not link yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement a Basilisk-facing wrapper surface around the imported ARM support unit: export `flush_icache`, `jit_abort`, `compiler_use_jit`, `m68k_compile_execute`, and add adapter overloads/wrappers for generated compemu entry points like `readbyte/writebyte/readword/writeword/readlong/writelong/calc_disp_ea_020` with the exact signatures expected by `compemu.cpp`.
asi.failure_signature: AArch64 split path now compiles and fails at link time: unresolved `flush_icache`, `jit_abort(char const*, ...)`, `compiler_use_jit`, `m68k_compile_execute`, and many generated compemu API symbols such as `readbyte(int,int,int)`, `writebyte(int,int,int)`, `readword`, `writeword`, `readlong`, `writelong`, `calc_disp_ea_020`.

description: Recreated the arm-specific support split skeleton plus Basilisk wrapper layer, but the first rebuild still fails immediately on imported-file include assumptions: compemu_support_arm.cpp still pulls missing UAE-only headers like sysconfig.h before reaching the prior compile/link frontier.

asi.hypothesis: Restoring the arm-specific support unit plus a thin Basilisk wrapper/export layer would get back near the previous link-only failure point and let wrapper fixes matter.
asi.rollback_reason: No score improvement; the import still fails at the first include boundary before the deeper ABI/link mismatches.
asi.next_action_hint: Strip remaining UAE-only header dependencies from compemu_support_arm.cpp/compemu_arm.h (start with sysconfig.h/options.h-era assumptions), then rerun to expose the next concrete compile blocker.
asi.failure_signature: fatal error: sysconfig.h: No such file or directory from compemu_support_arm.cpp line 46 during obj/compemu_support.o build

description: Stripped the first missing UAE-only include layer from the imported arm support path; the build now gets into the copied support/header body, where the next concrete blockers are type/macro mismatches (`STATIC_INLINE`, `fptype`) plus bad `uint8`/`uint32` compatibility defines causing recursive `uae_u*` expansion.

asi.hypothesis: After removing the top-level UAE-only includes, the arm-support split would progress far enough to expose the next real compatibility layer instead of dying on missing headers.
asi.rollback_reason: No score improvement; build still fails before producing a binary.
asi.next_action_hint: Retry the import with a cleaner type/macro shim: drop the `uint8`/`uint32` aliases entirely, define `STATIC_INLINE` locally, and replace the imported `fptype` usage with Basilisk's existing `fpu_register`/`fpu/types.h` surface before rerunning.
asi.failure_signature: compemu_arm.h: STATIC_INLINE undefined; compemu_arm.h: typedef fptype fpu_register fails; compemu_support_arm.cpp `#define uint8 uae_u8` / `#define uint32 uae_u32` breaks because sysdeps already maps uae_u8/uae_u32 to uint8/uint32, leading to recursive type-expansion errors

description: Cleaned up the imported type/macro layer enough to compile much deeper into the arm support split; the remaining blockers are now concrete Basilisk-vs-UAE ABI gaps in helper names and runtime surfaces rather than generic header syntax.

asi.hypothesis: Removing the recursive uint aliases and replacing the missing inline/FPU typedef assumptions would let the imported arm support compile far enough to expose the next real integration seams.
asi.rollback_reason: Still no build_ok improvement; compile now fails later on unresolved Basilisk compatibility gaps.
asi.next_action_hint: Patch the next focused ABI set instead of broad shims: add endian helper aliases (`uae_bswap_16/32`), map imported FPU/regstruct expectations (`regs.fp[].fp`, raw untranslated-opcount storage), replace UAE-only helpers (`ua`, `xfree`, `set_special`, `do_extra_cycles`, `CYCLE_UNIT`) with Basilisk equivalents, and bypass/remove the NOFLAGS-support-only `nfctbl` path if it is inactive here.
asi.failure_signature: Deeper compile now fails on `uae_bswap_16/32`, `regs.fp[i].fp`, `raw_cputbl_count` storage, `nfctbl`/`nfcpufunctbl` noflags table assumptions, `ua`/`xfree`, `set_special`, `CYCLE_UNIT`, and `do_extra_cycles`, plus the wrapper should use the existing external `m68k_do_compile_execute` declaration instead of a static redefinition.

description: Patched the next Basilisk-vs-UAE compatibility batch (endian aliases, FPU/regstruct mapping, untranslated-opcount storage, NOFLAGS-table guardrails, cycle-accounting fallback), which gets the arm-specific support split back through compilation into final link again.

asi.hypothesis: Addressing the concrete helper/ABI gaps from the prior deep compile failure would restore the arm-support split to the link-only frontier, proving the remaining blockers are wrapper/export symbols rather than parser mismatches.
asi.rollback_reason: Score stayed 0 because the binary still does not link yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the latest link-only surface: make `flush_icache` renaming survive LTO, provide/export `register_branch`, `prepare_for_call_1/2`, and satisfy runtime globals like `canbang`/`excep_env` using Basilisk-owned definitions instead of UAE-only assumptions.
asi.failure_signature: Link now fails on `arm_flush_icache_impl`, `excep_env`, `prepare_for_call_1/2`, `canbang`, and many `register_branch(unsigned int,unsigned int,unsigned char)` references after compemu_support.o compiles successfully.

description: Tried the next link-focused wrapper pass (local ARM flush pointer, no-TRY execute wrapper, 32-bit register_branch overload), but the rebuild regressed earlier because the `canbang` macro alias collided with the Basilisk prefs shim before reaching link.

asi.hypothesis: Providing the remaining link-only wrappers and replacing UAE runtime globals with Basilisk-local definitions would push the arm-support split closer to a linked binary.
asi.rollback_reason: No score improvement; this attempt reintroduced an earlier compile failure through a bad macro interaction.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the link-focused plan, but avoid preprocessor aliasing for `canbang`: either use a real `bool canbang = true` definition in the imported TU or keep the prefs shim free of that identifier, then rerun and inspect the remaining unresolved `prepare_for_call_*`/wrapper symbols.
asi.failure_signature: compemu_support_arm.cpp `#define canbang 1` collides with `const bool canbang = true;` in compemu_prefs.cpp, failing at obj/compemu_support.o before link.

description: Reapplied the arm-support split with a real Basilisk-owned `canbang` symbol and the latest wrapper/ABI shims; the build now reaches final link with the blocker set narrowed to just the missing call-clobber helpers `prepare_for_call_1/2`.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing the fragile `canbang` macro with a real symbol while keeping the helper/link wrapper batch intact would restore the link-only frontier and isolate the next missing export precisely.
asi.rollback_reason: No score improvement yet; the binary still fails to link.
asi.next_action_hint: Define or expose `prepare_for_call_1()` and `prepare_for_call_2()` in the AArch64 split path with external linkage or wrapper thunks matching what `compemu_midfunc_arm64_2.cpp` expects, then rerun immediately.
asi.failure_signature: Final link now only fails on `prepare_for_call_1()` and `prepare_for_call_2()` referenced by `jnf_MEM_READMEMBANK` / `jnf_MEM_WRITEMEMBANK` in compemu_midfunc_arm64_2.cpp; imported compemu_support_arm.cpp warns both are used but never defined.

description: Restored the arm-specific AArch64 support split enough to produce a linked BasiliskII binary again; JIT-enabled configure+make now succeeds, raising the score from 0 to 30 even though both smoke runs still exit immediately on missing prefs-file handling.

asi.hypothesis: Reapplying the arm-support split with a real `canbang` symbol plus the focused Basilisk/UAE ABI/link shims would convert the build gate from compile/link failure into a runnable binary.
asi.build_breakthrough: configure and final link both succeed for --enable-aarch64-jit-experimental
asi.runtime_failure_signature: both jit=false and jit=true exit with code 1 and log `ERROR: Could not load prefs file from .../prefs (No such file or directory)` before opening a window
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate BasiliskII `--config` handling and prefs loading semantics; make the smoke invocation tolerate a non-existent config path by starting from defaults plus CLI overrides, without regressing normal explicit-config behavior.

description: Taught Unix `--config` handling to create a missing prefs file at the requested path instead of exiting, which removed the earlier prefs-file startup error but immediately exposed a much worse regression: both jit=false and jit=true now segfault during startup before a window appears.

asi.hypothesis: Letting `--config <missing-path>` bootstrap defaults in place would unblock the smoke harness and allow the newly linked binary to reach window creation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed from 30 to 0 because both runtime modes now crash (exit 139), adding two crash penalties.
asi.next_action_hint: Debug the new early startup crash before keeping any prefs-loader change: capture a backtrace or inspect first-fault logs around prefs/bootstrap and JIT initialization, and compare with the original non-crashing 'missing prefs file' behavior.
asi.failure_signature: Both jit modes now log `Prefs file ... not found, creating new one there` and then exit 139 with no window; crash_count=2.

description: Tried to run a direct gdb reproduction with a precreated prefs file, but autoresearch mode rejected the custom command because this project requires `./autoresearch.sh` for experiment runs.

asi.hypothesis: A direct gdb run with a precreated prefs file would reveal the first runtime crash site behind the current missing-prefs harness blocker.
asi.rollback_reason: No benchmark data was collected; the experiment runner rejected non-harness commands in autoresearch mode.
asi.next_action_hint: Use bash/read for off-benchmark diagnostics, then return to `./autoresearch.sh` for scored runs; if needed, add temporary crash logging in code and evaluate via the harness.
asi.failure_signature: run_experiment rejected custom gdb command: `autoresearch.sh exists — you must run it instead of a custom command`

description: Combined two runtime-unblock changes: missing `--config` paths now bootstrap a prefs file, and AArch64 Unix RAM/ROM mapping no longer forces `VM_MAP_32BIT`. This gets `jit=false` far enough to open a Basilisk window and keep running, but the harness times out because the emulator does not exit on the harness SIGTERM, so the full benchmark still cannot complete.

asi.hypothesis: Allowing missing config bootstrap plus dropping the low-4GB RAM/ROM requirement on AArch64 would unblock the real runtime path hidden behind the earlier prefs-file exit and vm_acquire failure.
asi.rollback_reason: No validated primary improvement yet; the benchmark never completed because the process stayed alive after the harness tried to terminate it.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply these two changes and add explicit SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown handling so the smoke harness can stop Basilisk cleanly after 20s; then rerun to collect real off/on metrics.
asi.partial_evidence: `jit_off` created a prefs file, opened an 800x600 `Basilisk II` window under Xvfb, and stayed alive until the outer experiment timeout killed Xvfb.
asi.failure_signature: run_experiment timed out after 1200s with `jit_off` still running; artifact shows window `0x200008 "Basilisk II"` in xwininfo tree and basilisk.log ends with `X connection to :99 broken` after forced teardown.

description: Retried the runtime-unblock pair and added a Unix SIGTERM handler plus a safer NULL guard in `QuitEmulator()`. The emulator still ignores the harness shutdown path, so `jit=false` opens a Basilisk window and keeps running but the benchmark times out before it can score or reach `jit=true`.

asi.hypothesis: Missing-config bootstrap plus AArch64 high-address RAM mapping were the real runtime blockers, and a simple SIGTERM-to-quit hook would let the smoke harness finish cleanly.
asi.rollback_reason: No validated primary improvement yet; despite the visible window, the benchmark still times out in `jit_off` and never collects full off/on metrics.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat this as a harness robustness issue next: add a forced-kill fallback in `autoresearch.sh` after the 20s observation window so long-lived successful runs can be scored, then rerun on the current kept code.
asi.partial_evidence: `jit_off` again created the prefs file, opened `Basilisk II` under Xvfb, and remained alive even after manual SIGTERM.
asi.failure_signature: run_experiment timed out after 1200s with `jit_off` still alive; manual `kill -TERM` left the BasiliskII process running, and no `result.env` was written.
